Ingredients:
- 2 chicken breasts, cubed
- 2 cups cabbage, shredded
- 1 bell pepper, sliced
- 1 carrot, julienned
- 2 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 tbsp olive oil
- 1 tbsp low-sodium jerk seasoning
- ½ tsp thyme
- 1 tbsp coconut aminos (or low-sodium soy sauce)
- 1 lime wedge
- Fresh cilantro for garnish
Instructions:
1. Heat olive oil in large skillet or wok.
2. Add chicken cubes, cook 5-6 min until browned.
3. Add garlic, jerk seasoning, thyme, stir 1 min.
4. Add cabbage, bell pepper, carrot, stir-fry 4-5 min until crisp-tender.
5. Add coconut aminos, stir to combine.
6. Serve with lime wedge and cilantro garnish.
Insulin Benefits: Chicken provides lean protein for muscle preservation. Cabbage is high in fiber and antioxidants that improve insulin signaling. Coconut aminos add flavor without sugar. Low glycemic load with high volume for satiety.
Notes: Use lean chicken breast for lower fat. Can add scotch bonnet for heat.

Ingredients:
- 2 white fish fillets (snapper, tilapia)
- 1 cup pumpkin, cubed
- ½ cup okra, sliced
- 1 carrot, sliced
- ½ onion, chopped
- 2 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 inch ginger, grated
- 4 cups fish or vegetable broth
- 1 scotch bonnet pepper (whole, not pierced)
- ½ tsp thyme
- 2 scallions, chopped
- 1 tbsp lime juice
Instructions:
1. In pot, sauté onion, garlic, ginger until fragrant.
2. Add broth, bring to simmer.
3. Add pumpkin, carrot, okra, scotch bonnet, thyme.
4. Simmer 15 min until vegetables tender.
5. Add fish fillets, simmer 8-10 min until cooked.
6. Remove scotch bonnet, add scallions, lime juice.
7. Serve hot in bowls.
Insulin Benefits: Fish provides omega-3s that reduce inflammation. Pumpkin and okra are high-fiber, low-glycemic vegetables. Ginger may improve insulin sensitivity. Warm broth aids digestion and satiety.
Notes: Scotch bonnet adds flavor without heat if kept whole. Remove before serving.

Ingredients:
- 1 cup ackee, drained
- ½ cup firm tofu, crumbled
- ½ bell pepper, diced
- ¼ onion, diced
- 1 tomato, diced
- 1 scallion, chopped
- 1 tbsp olive oil
- ½ tsp turmeric
- ¼ tsp black pepper
- Pinch of paprika
- Fresh thyme leaves
Instructions:
1. Heat olive oil in skillet.
2. Sauté onion, bell pepper 3 min.
3. Add tofu, turmeric, black pepper, paprika.
4. Cook 5 min until tofu starts to brown.
5. Gently add ackee, tomato, thyme.
6. Cook 3-4 min until heated through.
7. Garnish with scallion.
Insulin Benefits: Tofu provides plant-based protein and isoflavones that may improve insulin sensitivity. Ackee is low in carbs and high in healthy fats. Turmeric has anti-inflammatory curcumin. No cholesterol, low saturated fat.
Notes: Great vegan alternative to traditional ackee and saltfish.

Ingredients:
- 500g lean goat meat, cubed
- 1 head cauliflower, riced
- 1 onion, chopped
- 3 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 tbsp curry powder
- 1 tsp turmeric
- ½ tsp cumin
- 1 cup coconut milk (light)
- 1 cup water or broth
- 2 potatoes, cubed (optional, for higher carb)
- 1 carrot, sliced
- 1 tbsp olive oil
- Fresh cilantro for garnish
Instructions:
1. Marinate goat meat with curry powder, turmeric, cumin for 30 min.
2. Heat olive oil in pot, brown goat meat.
3. Add onion, garlic, cook until soft.
4. Add coconut milk, water, bring to simmer.
5. Add carrots (and potatoes if using).
6. Cover, simmer 30-40 min until meat tender.
7. Meanwhile, pulse cauliflower in food processor, sauté 5 min.
8. Serve curry over cauliflower rice, garnish with cilantro.
Insulin Benefits: Goat meat is leaner than beef with more protein per calorie. Cauliflower rice replaces high-glycemic white rice. Curry spices contain anti-inflammatory compounds. Coconut milk provides medium-chain triglycerides.
Nutrition Notes: ~420 cal | 40g protein | 22g fat | 18g carbs | 7g fiber (without potatoes)
Notes: For lower carb, omit potatoes. Can use beef or chicken if goat unavailable.

Ingredients:
- 6 egg whites
- 2 whole eggs
- 2 cups callaloo (or spinach), chopped
- ½ bell pepper, diced
- ¼ onion, diced
- ½ cup mushrooms, sliced
- 1 oz feta cheese, crumbled (optional)
- 1 tbsp olive oil
- ½ tsp black pepper
- Pinch of nutmeg
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. Heat olive oil in oven-safe skillet.
3. Sauté onion, bell pepper, mushrooms 5 min.
4. Add callaloo, cook until wilted.
5. Whisk egg whites, whole eggs, black pepper, nutmeg.
6. Pour eggs over vegetables, sprinkle feta.
7. Cook on stovetop 2-3 min until edges set.
8. Transfer to oven, bake 12-15 min until set.
9. Let cool 5 min before slicing.
Insulin Benefits: Egg whites provide high-quality protein with minimal fat. Callaloo is rich in iron, magnesium, and fiber. Mushrooms contain chromium which may improve insulin action. Low calorie, high volume.
Notes: Can make ahead and reheat. Omit feta for dairy-free.

Ingredients:
- 2 white fish fillets (snapper, parrot fish)
- 1 cho-cho (chayote), sliced
- 1 carrot, sliced
- ½ onion, sliced
- 2 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 inch ginger, sliced
- 2 scallions, chopped
- 1 tomato, sliced
- 1 tbsp olive oil
- ½ tsp thyme
- 1 lime, juiced
- 1 cup vegetable broth
Instructions:
1. In steamer basket, layer cho-cho, carrot, onion.
2. Place fish fillets on top of vegetables.
3. Scatter garlic, ginger, scallions, tomato over fish.
4. Drizzle olive oil, lime juice, sprinkle thyme.
5. Pour broth into steamer base.
6. Steam 15-20 min until fish flakes easily.
7. Serve fish with steamed vegetables and broth.
Insulin Benefits: Steaming preserves nutrients without adding fat. Cho-cho is very low in carbs and calories. Ginger and garlic have anti-diabetic properties. Light meal that won't spike blood sugar overnight.
Notes: Can use any firm white fish. Save broth for sipping.

Ingredients:
- 1 cup kidney beans, cooked
- 2 cups mixed greens (callaloo, spinach)
- ½ avocado, sliced
- ½ cucumber, diced
- ¼ red onion, thinly sliced
- 1 hard-boiled egg, sliced
- 2 tbsp pumpkin seeds
- Dressing: 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tbsp lime juice, ½ tsp mustard, black pepper
Instructions:
1. Arrange greens in bowls.
2. Top with kidney beans, avocado, cucumber, onion.
3. Add egg slices, sprinkle pumpkin seeds.
4. Whisk dressing ingredients, drizzle over bowl.
5. Toss gently before eating.
Insulin Benefits: Beans provide soluble fiber that slows glucose absorption. Avocado adds healthy monounsaturated fats. Pumpkin seeds are rich in magnesium, crucial for insulin function. Complete meal with balanced macros.
Notes: Use canned beans (rinsed) for convenience. Can add tuna or chicken for more protein.

Ingredients:
- 3 chicken thighs (skinless, bone-in)
- 1 sweet potato, cubed
- 1 carrot, cubed
- ½ pumpkin, cubed
- 1 onion, quartered
- 4 garlic cloves, whole
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- 1 tbsp rosemary, chopped
- 1 tsp paprika
- ½ tsp black pepper
- 1 lemon, quartered
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. Toss vegetables with 1 tbsp olive oil, rosemary, paprika, pepper.
3. Place vegetables in roasting pan.
4. Rub chicken with remaining oil, season with pepper.
5. Nestle chicken among vegetables, add lemon quarters.
6. Roast 35-40 min until chicken cooked through.
7. Let rest 5 min before serving.
Insulin Benefits: Chicken thighs provide iron and zinc for metabolic health. Root vegetables roasted have lower glycemic impact than boiled. Rosemary contains antioxidants that may improve insulin sensitivity. One-pan meal for easy cleanup.
Notes: Use skinless chicken to reduce fat. Can use breast for leaner option.

Ingredients:
- 1 cup spinach
- ½ avocado
- ½ cucumber
- ½ cup coconut water
- 1 scoop protein powder (unflavored or vanilla)
- Toppings: 2 tbsp chia seeds, ¼ cup berries, 1 tbsp coconut flakes, 5 almonds
Instructions:
1. Blend spinach, avocado, cucumber, coconut water until smooth.
2. Add protein powder, blend briefly.
3. Pour into bowl.
4. Top with chia seeds, berries, coconut flakes, almonds.
5. Eat with spoon.
Insulin Benefits: Spinach provides magnesium and nitrates for blood flow. Avocado adds healthy fats for sustained energy. Protein powder supports muscle repair without spiking insulin. Berries are low-glycemic fruits with antioxidants.
Notes: Great for hot days or after exercise. Adjust thickness with more/less coconut water.

Meal Name: Stuffed Bell Peppers with Ground Turkey
Ingredients:
- 4 bell peppers, tops removed, seeds cleaned
- 500g ground turkey (lean)
- ½ cup cauliflower rice
- ¼ onion, diced
- 2 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 tomato, diced
- 1 tsp cumin
- ½ tsp chili powder
- ½ cup tomato sauce (no sugar added)
- ¼ cup cilantro, chopped
- ½ cup shredded cheese (optional)
Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. Brown ground turkey with onion, garlic.
3. Add cauliflower rice, tomato, spices, cook 5 min.
4. Stir in tomato sauce, cilantro.
5. Stuff peppers with mixture.
6. Place in baking dish, add ¼ cup water to dish.
7. Top with cheese if using.
8. Bake 25-30 min until peppers tender.
Insulin Benefits: Turkey is lean protein that promotes satiety. Bell peppers are high in vitamin C and low in carbs. Cauliflower rice adds bulk without starch. Spices like cumin may improve glucose metabolism.
Notes: Can use ground chicken or beef. Omit cheese for dairy-free.
